llms-full.txt — Full documentation concatenation for token-constrained agents or offline crawling.

This is an auto-generated file. Regenerate periodically by running:
  node scripts/generate-llms-full.js > public/llms-full.txt

See llms-full-generator.ts (bottom of this file) for the script.
-->

# Acme Agent Tools — Full Documentation

<!--- llms-full.txt auto-generated 2026-04-17 from 18 pages, ~52k tokens --->

---

## Table of contents

1. Getting started
2. SDK reference
3. MCP server
4. API reference
5. Discovery
6. Advanced topics

---

## 1. Getting started

### Installation

Acme Agent Tools runs on Node 20+ and pnpm.

```sh
pnpm add @acme/sdk
```

For a monorepo:

```sh
# Clone the repo
git clone https://github.com/acme/agent-tools.git
cd agent-tools

# Install dependencies
pnpm install

# Start development servers
pnpm dev
```

Runs at:
- SDK playground: http://localhost:5173
- API server: http://localhost:3001
- Dashboard: http://localhost:3000

### Quick start: build your first agent

```typescript
import { Agent, Tool } from '@acme/sdk';

// 1. Define a tool
const getTodaysTasks = Tool.define({
  name: 'get_tasks',
  description: 'Retrieve tasks for the current day',
  parameters: {
    filter: { type: 'string', enum: ['all', 'urgent'] },
  },
  handler: async ({ filter }) => {
    return [
      { id: 1, title: 'Review PR', priority: 'high' },
      { id: 2, title: 'Write docs', priority: 'normal' },
    ].filter(t => filter === 'all' || t.priority === 'high');
  },
});

// 2. Create an agent
const agent = new Agent({
  name: 'TaskMaster',
  description: 'Helps you manage your daily tasks',
  tools: [getTodaysTasks],
});

// 3. Query the agent
const result = await agent.query({
  prompt: 'What are my urgent tasks?',
});

console.log(result);
// Output: { success: true, data: [...] }
```

---

## 2. SDK reference

### Agent class

```typescript
import { Agent, Tool } from '@acme/sdk';

const agent = new Agent({
  name: string;                    // Identifier (lowercase, hyphenated)
  description: string;             // One-liner for agent introspection
  tools: Tool[];                   // Array of Tool definitions
  model?: 'opus-4-7' | 'sonnet-4-6';  // Default: opus-4-7
  systemPrompt?: string;           // Optional system instructions
  mcpServers?: MCPServerConfig[];  // Remote MCP servers
});

// Query the agent
await agent.query({
  prompt: string;              // User message
  messages?: Message[];        // Conversation history (optional)
  maxTurns?: number;           // Max tool-call iterations (default: 10)
  thinking?: boolean;          // Enable extended thinking (Opus only)
  thinkingBudget?: number;     // Token budget for reasoning
}): Promise<AgentResult>
```

### Tool definition

Tools are the interface between agents and services. Define with Zod schemas:

```typescript
import { Tool } from '@acme/sdk';
import { z } from 'zod';

const searchUsers = Tool.define({
  name: 'search_users',
  description: 'Find users by email or name. Use when agent needs to identify or lookup a specific user.',
  parameters: z.object({
    query: z.string().describe('Search term (email or full name)'),
    limit: z.number().int().positive().default(10).describe('Max results to return'),
    include_archived: z.boolean().default(false).describe('Include archived users?'),
  }),
  handler: async ({ query, limit, include_archived }) => {
    // Your implementation
    return users;
  },
});
```

**Tool naming:** Namespace with service name. `search_users`, `create_invoice`, not `search` or `create`.

**Parameters:** All fields explicit; no implicit context. Provide examples in descriptions.

**Handler:** Async function; return semantic data, not opaque IDs. Errors should be data in result, not thrown.

### Tool result shape

```typescript
type ToolResult =
  | { success: true; data: unknown; tokens?: number }
  | {
      success: false;
      error: {
        type: string;           // RFC 9457: 'validation_error', 'not_found', 'auth_required'
        status: number;         // HTTP status code
        detail: string;         // Human-readable error
        instance?: string;      // URI identifying the specific occurrence
      };
    };
```

Example error response:

```typescript
{
  success: false,
  error: {
    type: 'validation_error',
    status: 400,
    detail: 'Email is required and must be valid format.',
    instance: '/api/users/create#email',
  }
}
```

---

## 3. MCP server

### Server setup

MCP (Model Context Protocol) 2025-11-25 servers expose tools, resources, and prompts to agents.

```typescript
// server.ts
import { MCPServer } from '@acme/sdk/mcp';
import { createToolHandlers } from './tools';

const server = new MCPServer({
  name: 'my-service',
  version: '1.0.0',
  capabilities: ['tools', 'resources'],
});

// Register tools
const tools = createToolHandlers();
server.tools.register(tools);

// Start server (stdio transport)
server.start();
```

### Tool registration

```typescript
server.tools.register({
  name: 'user_search',
  description: 'Search users by email or name',
  inputSchema: {
    type: 'object',
    properties: {
      query: { type: 'string', description: 'Search term' },
      limit: { type: 'integer', default: 10 },
    },
    required: ['query'],
    additionalProperties: false,  // Required for OpenAI strict: true
  },
  handler: async (input) => {
    // Validate input
    const { query, limit } = input;

    // Execute
    const results = await db.users.search(query, limit);

    // Return RFC 9457 shape
    return {
      success: true,
      data: results,
    };
  },
});
```

### Resource endpoints (MCP 2025-11-25)

Resources expose static or dynamic data that agents can read:

```typescript
server.resources.register({
  uri: 'memory://task-backlog',
  name: 'Task backlog',
  description: 'All pending tasks',
  mimeType: 'application/json',
  handler: async () => ({
    tasks: await db.tasks.list({ status: 'pending' }),
  }),
});
```

### OAuth 2.0 (M2M client credentials)

For remote agents to call your MCP server securely:

```typescript
// server.ts
server.auth.oauth2({
  tokenEndpoint: 'https://example.com/oauth/token',
  requiredScopes: ['agent:execute', 'agent:read'],
  validateToken: async (token) => {
    // Verify JWT: iss, aud, exp
    const claims = jwt.verify(token, PUBLIC_KEY);
    return claims.aud === 'agents' && claims.exp > Date.now();
  },
});

// Agent side (SDK)
const agent = new Agent({
  mcpServers: [{
    name: 'my-service',
    url: 'https://example.com/mcp',
    auth: {
      type: 'oauth2',
      clientId: 'my-agent',
      clientSecret: process.env.MCP_CLIENT_SECRET,
      grantType: 'client_credentials',
    },
  }],
});
```

---

## 4. API reference

### REST endpoints

All responses follow RFC 9457 `Problem+JSON` for errors.

#### POST /agents/:agentId/query

Query an agent with a prompt.

```
POST /agents/my-agent/query
Content-Type: application/json

{
  "prompt": "What are my tasks?",
  "maxTurns": 5
}

200 OK:
{
  "success": true,
  "data": {...},
  "tokensUsed": 1234
}

400 Bad Request:
{
  "type": "https://example.com/errors/validation",
  "title": "Validation Error",
  "status": 400,
  "detail": "Prompt is required and must be non-empty.",
  "instance": "/agents/my-agent/query"
}
```

#### GET /tools

List all available tools.

```
GET /tools

200 OK:
{
  "tools": [
    {
      "name": "user_search",
      "description": "...",
      "parameters": {...}
    },
    ...
  ]
}
```

#### POST /tools/:toolName/call

Directly invoke a tool (for debugging).

```
POST /tools/user_search/call
Content-Type: application/json

{
  "query": "alice@example.com",
  "limit": 5
}

200 OK:
{
  "success": true,
  "data": {...}
}
```

### Content negotiation

API supports markdown responses for agents with limited context:

```
GET /api/docs/getting-started
Accept: text/markdown

200 OK
Content-Type: text/markdown; charset=utf-8
Vary: Accept
x-markdown-tokens: 2500

# Getting Started

[Full markdown content...]
```

---

## 5. Discovery

### AGENTS.md

Standard agent context file. Read by Claude Code, Cursor, Copilot, and others.

```
https://example.com/AGENTS.md
https://github.com/acme/agent-tools/blob/main/AGENTS.md
```

See canonical spec: https://agents.md

### Agent card (A2A v1.0 RC)

If you run an MCP server or agent interface, advertise yourself at `/.well-known/agent.json`:

```json
GET /.well-known/agent.json

{
  "name": "Acme Agent Tools",
  "description": "API for agent-consumable services",
  "homepage": "https://example.com",
  "rpc_endpoint": {
    "url": "https://example.com/rpc",
    "protocol": "json-rpc-2.0"
  },
  "auth": {
    "type": "oauth2",
    "grant_type": "client_credentials",
    "token_endpoint": "https://example.com/oauth/token"
  },
  "capabilities": ["task_execution", "message_routing"],
  "version": "1.0.0"
}
```

Spec: https://a2a-protocol.org/latest/specification/

### robots.txt (AI crawlers)

Allow AI training and search bots; block admin paths:

```
# Allow AI training bots
User-agent: GPTBot
Disallow: /admin
Disallow: /private

User-agent: ClaudeBot
Disallow: /admin
Disallow: /private

User-agent: PerplexityBot
Disallow: /admin

# Block all other bots
User-agent: *
Disallow: /admin
Allow: /llms.txt
```

Spec: https://agents.md (Robots.txt section)

---

## 6. Advanced topics

### JSON-LD structured data

Embed machine-readable metadata in HTML for agent discovery:

```html
<script type="application/ld+json">
{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "SoftwareApplication",
  "name": "Acme Agent Tools",
  "description": "SDK and servers for agent integration",
  "url": "https://example.com",
  "codeRepository": "https://github.com/acme/agent-tools",
  "downloadUrl": "https://www.npmjs.com/package/@acme/sdk",
  "applicationCategory": "DeveloperApplication"
}
</script>
```

Types: `SoftwareApplication`, `WebAPI`, `APIReference`, `FAQPage`, `HowTo`.

### NLWeb integration

Expose a `/ask` endpoint for natural language queries:

```typescript
// app/api/ask/route.ts (Next.js)
export async function POST(req: Request) {
  const { query, max_sources } = await req.json();

  // Search docs, find relevant pages
  const results = await searchDocs(query);

  // Use LLM to generate answer
  const answer = await generateAnswer(query, results);

  return Response.json({
    answer: answer,
    sources: results.map(r => ({
      title: r.title,
      url: r.url,
      snippet: r.snippet,
    })),
  });
}
```

Spec: https://github.com/microsoft/NLWeb

### Error handling (RFC 9457)

All errors follow `Problem+JSON` format:

```typescript
// Validation error
{
  "type": "https://example.com/errors/validation",
  "title": "Validation Error",
  "status": 400,
  "detail": "Email must be valid format.",
  "instance": "/api/users/create#email",
  "errors": [
    { "field": "email", "message": "Invalid format" }
  ]
}

// Not found
{
  "type": "https://example.com/errors/not-found",
  "title": "Not Found",
  "status": 404,
  "detail": "User with id=123 does not exist.",
  "instance": "/api/users/123"
}

// Rate limit
{
  "type": "https://example.com/errors/rate-limit",
  "title": "Too Many Requests",
  "status": 429,
  "detail": "Rate limit exceeded. Retry after 60 seconds.",
  "retry_after": 60
}
```

Spec: https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9457.html

---

- [schema.org types](https://schema.org)

---

## Generate script

```typescript
// scripts/generate-llms-full.ts — Run via: npx ts-node scripts/generate-llms-full.ts

import fs from 'fs';
import path from 'path';
import glob from 'glob';

const docsDir = path.join(process.cwd(), 'docs');
const files = glob.sync('**/*.md', { cwd: docsDir }).sort();

let content = `# Acme Agent Tools — Full Documentation\n\n`;
content += `<!--- llms-full.txt auto-generated ${new Date().toISOString().split('T')[0]} from ${files.length} pages --->\n\n`;

for (const file of files) {
  const filePath = path.join(docsDir, file);
  const markdown = fs.readFileSync(filePath, 'utf-8');

  // Extract heading (H1 or H2)
  const heading = markdown.match(/^#{1,2}\s+(.+)/m)?.[1] || path.basename(file);
  content += `\n\n## ${heading}\n\n`;
  content += markdown.replace(/^# /, '### ');  // Shift headings down
}

process.stdout.write(content);
```

To auto-generate:
```sh
npx ts-node scripts/generate-llms-full.ts > public/llms-full.txt
```

Or add as npm script:
```json
{
  "scripts": {
    "docs:llms-full": "ts-node scripts/generate-llms-full.ts > public/llms-full.txt"
  }
}
```
